title: Root Insurance Parent Marks First Profitable Quarter
text: Root Inc., the parent company of auto insurtech Root Insurance, achieved profitability for the first time in company history during the third quarter of 2024. CEO Alex Timm described the milestone as a pivotal moment for Root, stating that This quarter validates the strength of our business model, our technology, and our customer value proposition. The company demonstrated significant growth metrics during Q3: Policies in force increased 57% to 407,313. Gross written premium rose 48% to $332 million. Net income reached $23 million (a $69 million year-over-year improvement). Operating income totaled $34 million. Adjusted EBITDA reached $42 million. Gross combined ratio of 89%. Timm credited the results to targeted acquisition investment, loss ratio performance, and the efficiency of our fixed expense base. The company ended the quarter with $439 million in unencumbered capital and minimal quarterly cash consumption of $8 million. Additionally, Root refinanced its term loan with BlackRock Capital Investment Advisors, reducing interest expense by approximately 50% on a run-rate basis. This refinancing is expected to further accelerate the company's profitability trajectory.
